title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth-place performance on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors that need four to five GPUs. The model supports efficient deployment on mobile devices, hospital infrastructure, and local machines via LM Studio, with Apache 2.0 licensing enabling faster procurement for sovereign institutions.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4, focusing on ownership through open models. The 31B variant ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while using half the GPU memory of comparable competitors, and it runs on single GPUs. Smaller 2B and 4B parameter variants fit on mobile devices, opening deployment across a range from phones to hospital systems. The takeaway: compression and inference optimization let open models compete near the top of the leaderboard while lowering hardware requirements.
